content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I remember my first massive disappointment.
You spend days searching for the perfect idea. Hours shooting and sleepless nights editing. You tweak every single frame. And then, with a sinking heart, you hit “Publish,” expecting the views to roll in, and… absolute silence.
Do you know what the problem was? My title was so perfectly correct and incredibly boring that not even my mom would have clicked on it.
I decided to delegate this problem to artificial intelligence. I opened ChatGPT and typed the classic: “Give me 5 titles for my video”. And it happily spat out something like: “Dive Into the Amazing World of Camera Settings”.
It was a total disaster. The AI either generated dull, robotic corporate-speak or descended into yellow clickbait like “YOU WON’T BELIEVE WHAT HAPPENED AT THE END!”.
Why Normal Commands Don’t Work
Are you absolutely sure the neural network even understands the difference between genuine intrigue and cheap clickbait that just annoys the viewer?
Most prompts online are just direct instructions. The AI simply has to guess what you mean by “good”. I spent weeks running tests just to understand one very simple thing: neural networks don’t need a command. They need context that completely eliminates ambiguity.
Instead of just asking it to “write clicky titles,” I decided to write out a full-fledged role for the
AI. I made it an expert in YouTube algorithms. I strictly outlined that it must use engagement triggers — like intrigue, emotions, benefits, and numbers — but categorically avoid the kind of clickbait that disappoints the audience. And most importantly, the titles had to easily adapt to different content formats.
When I finally entered this prompt, the result genuinely scared me a little. The machine started pumping out options that I would have spent two hours brainstorming myself.
The Prompt
↓ Copy the complete prompt below ↓
PROMPTS WITH YOUR DATA:
Imagine you're an expert at creating powerful YouTube video titles that grab attention, increase CTR, and promote your content in recommendations. You have a deep understanding of YouTube algorithms, the psychology of click-through rates, and A/B testing methods for titles.Your job is to develop headlines that:
- Capture attention and arouse curiosity, while still remaining relevant to the content of the video.
- Use engagement triggers: intrigue, emotions, shock, benefit, questions, numbers.
- Optimized for search queries and keywords to increase visibility.
- Encourage clicks, but avoid clickbait that frustrates the viewer.
- Adaptable to different content formats (entertainment, educational, reviews, challenges, trends).
You create titles that not only grab attention but also retain viewers, helping drive views, engagement, and video promotion through YouTube's algorithms.
Create 5 attention-grabbing headlines for this text: [copy and paste the video transcript]
Write 5 attention-grabbing titles for a YouTube video on [topic].
Give examples of attention-grabbing titles for YouTube videos.
[topic] that will make people want to click and watch the video.
Make them in the style of [the magazine or author].
Why I’m Giving This Away ?
Could you write a request like this yourself? Probably, yes.
But let me emphasize one thing: seeing a real, working example is infinitely more useful than reading yet another theoretical article about AI “best practices”. When I was just starting to mess around with neural networks, other people’s working prompts taught me more than any long, complicated guide ever did.
The absolute fastest way to learn is to take a prompt that actually works and ask yourself exactly why every single sentence is needed in there.
